stateSemantics.adopted: Selected without an assessment-specific topology change; this state alone is not evidence of a live integration.
stateSemantics.adapted: Selected with a documented assessment-specific boundary; this state alone is not evidence of a live integration.
stateSemantics.not_applicable: Excluded by the current architecture with an explicit trigger for reconsideration.
stateSemantics.exception_pending: Required or conditionally required evidence, access, or approval is unresolved and blocks the stated release claim.

evidence: Team Kit requires six justfile recipes and shared caller workflows.
The approved operating model limits assessment pull-request CI to validation only and denies deploy, inherited secrets, and OIDC in that workflow.
entries.trigger: COS-015 and COS-019 implement and test the six-recipe validation-only caller after the monorepo toolchain is frozen.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: Validation jobs receive source code only; provider credentials, repository secrets, OIDC tokens, and deployment authority are excluded.
entries.releaseConsequence: The release may claim only validation-only CI after the caller is implemented and exercised; it may not claim the organization shared workflow or deployment path is live from this decision record.

entries.owner: Observability owner with Spring Oaks repository authority
evidence: Team Kit requires every emitted custom metric to be registered in Lexicon.
No external repository access or mutation is authorized in COS-008, and no application metric exists in this pre-scaffold wave.
entries.trigger: Before COS-013 metric implementation or any release claim, obtain exact repository authority or a separately approved assessment exception with a metric-to-registry ledger.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: Metric definitions must contain aggregate operational metadata only and must not contain message content, personal identifiers, or credential values.
entries.releaseConsequence: A release emitting unregistered custom metrics cannot claim full Golden Path conformance; the exception must close or remain an explicit release limitation.

entries.owner: Observability owner with Main Dashboard repository authority
evidence: Team Kit requires every emitted custom metric to have a Main Dashboard widget.
No external dashboard access or mutation is authorized in COS-008, and no application metric exists in this pre-scaffold wave.
entries.trigger: Before COS-013 metric implementation or any release claim, obtain exact dashboard authority or a separately approved assessment exception tied to the same metric ledger as Lexicon.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: Dashboard widgets may expose aggregate service and SLA measures only; executive content, raw tenant identifiers, and secrets are prohibited.
entries.releaseConsequence: A release emitting metrics without verified dashboard coverage cannot claim full Golden Path observability conformance.

evidence: Team Kit requires production critical failures to page PagerDuty and each DLQ to use one self-resolving alarm.
No PagerDuty service, routing authority, secret reference, or external mutation is verified or authorized in COS-008.
entries.trigger: Before any production effect-capable release, verify service ownership and a Secrets Manager reference, synthesize the single-alarm lifecycle, and exercise trigger plus automatic resolution under the deployment gates.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: Incident payloads must contain opaque correlation IDs and safe operational summaries only; message bodies, recipients, tenant identifiers, and routing keys are excluded.
entries.releaseConsequence: Critical production paths remain release-blocked for full Golden Path conformance until the live alert lifecycle is verified or a separately approved non-production exception applies.

entries.capability: GitHub Actions AWS OIDC deployment authentication
evidence: Team Kit shared deployment workflows require id-token write permission and target-account bootstrap context.
The approved assessment pull-request workflow is validation-only; account identity, qualifier, trust policy, and deployment authority are intentionally not inspected in COS-008.
entries.trigger: Before enabling any CI deployment, separately verify target account, role trust, least-privilege policy, region, bootstrap qualifier, environment binding, and exact deployment approval.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: OIDC must provide short-lived, audience-bound credentials; static AWS access keys, administrator exports, and credential values in repository or workflow state are prohibited.
entries.releaseConsequence: CI must not request an OIDC token or deploy while this exception is pending; deployment conformance cannot be claimed from validation-only CI.

entries.sourceContract: plan Team Kit applicability decision for agent turns
evidence: The approved architecture makes LangSmith conditional on package compatibility, account access, redaction, retention, and data-handling approval.
No account, retention setting, data-processing terms, or sanitized trace contract is verified in COS-008.
entries.trigger: Before exporting any agent trace, approve a field allowlist, retention and deletion policy, tenant boundary, package compatibility, account access, and a synthetic-data validation run.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: Executive messages, attachments, provider tokens, raw prompts, and personal identifiers must not leave AWS without explicit authorization and a verified redaction contract.
entries.releaseConsequence: LangSmith remains disabled; CloudWatch evidence must not be labeled equivalent, and prompt-iteration conformance cannot cite LangSmith until the exception closes.

entries.capability: Team Kit Chat SDK Asana-triggered agent surface
entries.sourceContract: Team Kit build-ai-agents Chat SDK and Asana applicability clause
evidence: The approved core ingests communication-provider events and models Asana through a sibling WorkManagementConnector.
No Asana-triggered conversational agent surface is selected for the core product.
entries.trigger: Reassess only if an approved Asana-triggered agent/chat surface is added; if selected, use the Team Kit adapter and state pattern rather than a hand-built bot.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: No Chat SDK state or external data flow exists in the selected core architecture.
entries.releaseConsequence: The release must not claim Chat SDK adoption; its absence does not block the current connector-and-approval architecture.

entries.sourceContract: Team Kit build-ai-agents memory applicability clause
entries.owner: Chief knowledge and agent architecture owner
evidence: Canonical messages, threads, authorized retrieval snapshots, and audit records are the system of record.
No separate Chat SDK conversational session requiring AgentCore Memory is selected for the core product.
entries.trigger: Reassess only if a distinct conversational-agent session with lifecycle semantics not covered by canonical communication history is approved.
entries.dataHandlingImpact: No duplicate memory store or additional executive-content retention boundary is introduced.
entries.releaseConsequence: The release must not claim AgentCore Memory adoption; adding it later requires a retention, deletion, tenancy, and source-of-truth decision.
